blockingIssues.description: The original feature commit 5468204 did not guarantee chronological pool snapshots: fetchUniswapV3PoolSnapshots requested poolDayDatas with orderDirection desc and returned them unsorted, so downstream consumers could receive reverse-chronological liquidity/volume history. Current HEAD fixes this later, but the reviewed feature itself did not fully satisfy its snapshot contract.

Current HEAD still leaves a contract gap in token-detail/info live pricing. fetchLiveSimpleTokenPrice only returns live values for the narrow hardcoded token set mapped by findCoinIdForToken, so other Ethereum token addresses discoverable from onchain pools still fall back to seeded pool pricing instead of reflecting live DeFiLlama pricing as the feature contract required.

The compare-suite timeout regression was fixed with test isolation, but the assigned feature also required the handoff commit to fully contain the claimed implementation. Commit 3aebd7e is test-only, so scrutiny still cannot validate the claimed live onchain catalog/runtime implementation from this feature commit alone.

This scrutiny-proof feature still fails its own attribution requirement. Its handoff points at commit d16f2c4, which contains only scrutiny artifacts and environment notes, while the real no-timeout-masking and current-head proof work lives in other commits. That leaves the commit attribution ambiguity unresolved even though current HEAD behavior is now correct.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the onchain-api-worker procedure for scrutiny-fix and follow-up features whose acceptance criteria include proof/attribution requirements, not just runtime code changes. Workers should not report success when the recorded commitId does not actually contain the implementation the feature claims to deliver.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Two failed reviews in this round were attribution failures rather than behavior failures: remove-timeout-masking-from-onchain-compare-coverage (commit 3aebd7e is test-only) and prove-live-onchain-pool-catalog-without-timeout-masking (handoff commit d16f2c4 contains only scrutiny artifacts).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit guidance for provider/time-series features to verify ordering invariants on history-style endpoints before handoff.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: thegraph-provider-module originally shipped pool snapshots in reverse chronological order and only later hardening work plus review caught the missing ascending-order contract.

rejectedObservations.observation: The shared architecture note says all onchain routes are fixture-backed, which is stale relative to current live DeFiLlama/The Graph behavior.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in prior rounds and outside this validator's additive-update scope

rejectedObservations.observation: Mission/shared-state docs do not record the corrected DeFiLlama /yields/pools contract even though later fixes depended on it.
rejectedObservations.reason: knowledge gap is non-blocking and outside this validator's additive-update scope

rejectedObservations.observation: Reviewer prompts reference .factory/library/architecture.md in locations that may not exist in every environment.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous ownership and outside this validator's additive-update scope
